data.title: Bitcoin's Future Outlook: Potential Consolidation and Long-Term Growth
data.description: Bitcoin is anticipated to consolidate within a $75,000 to $100,000 range during the first half of the year, according to NS3.AI. Bitwise CIO Matt Hougan suggests this stability will be influenced by regulatory clarity and the digestion of macroeconomic risks. He predicts a potential breakout later in the year as institutional understanding and broader adoption of Bitcoin improve. Looking further ahead, Hougan forecasts that Bitcoin could reach approximately $6.5 million per coin over the next 20 years. This growth is expected to be driven primarily by global debt expansion and currency devaluation, rather than accelerated adoption.

data.title: Bloomberg: Nomura's European business turned to a loss due to the decline in crypto assets and has tightened its management of positions and risk exposure.
data.description: A Nomura Holdings executive said the company has strengthened risk controls on its cryptocurrency business after its European operations turned unprofitable due to setbacks in the digital asset market. Chief Financial Officer Hiroyuki Moriuchi said on Friday's conference call reviewing quarterly results: "We have tightened management of our positions and risk exposure to mitigate short-term profit volatility." He added that Japan's largest brokerage firm has reduced its virtual currency holdings but remains committed to the long-term development of its cryptocurrency business. (Bloomberg)

data.title: Cryptocurrency Market Sees Mixed Performance with Notable Gains and Losses
data.description:
On January 31, several cryptocurrencies experienced significant fluctuations in their market values. According to PANews, the top gainers of the day included NOT, which is currently priced at $0.000504, marking a 4.80% increase. STX followed with a price of $0.291, rising by 2.46%, while STRK is now at $0.0616, up by 2.29%. JUP and FLOW also saw gains, with JUP at $0.207, increasing by 1.82%, and FLOW at $0.0665, up by 1.49%.

Conversely, some cryptocurrencies faced declines. AXS is now priced at $1.953, experiencing a decrease of 8.86%. ZRO followed with a price of $1.822, down by 6.95%. ENJ, ILV, and HBAR also saw declines, with ENJ at $0.0269, dropping by 3.37%, ILV at $4.903, down by 3.06%, and HBAR at $0.0984, decreasing by 2.33%.

data.title: Bitwise's Chief Investment Officer: Bitcoin will trade sideways between $75,000 and $100,000 in the first half of the year, with a long-term bullish outlook of $6.5 million.
data.description: In an interview, Bitwise Chief Investment Officer Matt Hougan stated that he expects Bitcoin to trade sideways between $75,000 and $100,000 in the first half of this year, but a breakout is more likely later in the year as the regulatory environment becomes clearer and macroeconomic risks are digested. Hougan reiterated his view that Bitcoin could reach approximately $6.5 million per coin within the next 20 years. Hougan stated that the core assumption is not accelerated adoption, but rather continued growth in global debt, increased money supply, and currency devaluation. Hougan believes Bitcoin is an upgraded version of gold, and central banks are only just beginning to understand its role. "As long as the future doesn't change much from the past 15 years, we can achieve our goal. It's just a matter of time." (CoinDesk)

data.title: U.S. Senate Passes Bill Ahead of House Vote Amid Potential Government Shutdown
data.description: The U.S. Senate has approved a new bill, with the House of Representatives scheduled to conduct its final vote on February 2. There is a possibility of a partial government shutdown on January 31, which could lead to increased uncertainty in policy. Jack Kong, CEO of Nano Labs, posted on X that such uncertainty often drives funds towards safe-haven assets like Bitcoin. Historically, during government shutdowns, the cryptocurrency market has experienced volatility as investors seek decentralized assets to hedge against risks in the traditional financial system. In the short term, attention is focused on changes in market sentiment.

data.title: Nomura Holdings Tightens Risk Control in Cryptocurrency Business
data.description: Nomura Holdings has intensified risk management in its cryptocurrency operations following losses in its European business due to setbacks in the digital asset market. According to BlockBeats, Chief Financial Officer Hiroyuki Moriuchi stated during a quarterly earnings call on Friday that the company has tightened its management of holdings and risk exposure to curb short-term profit fluctuations. Despite reducing its virtual currency holdings, Japan's largest brokerage remains committed to the long-term development of its cryptocurrency business.

data.title: Whale Sells UNI Holdings After Five Years, Profits $1.72 Million
data.description: A significant cryptocurrency holder has sold 2.493 million UNI tokens, valued at approximately $10.62 million, after holding them for five years. According to Foresight News, this sale resulted in a profit of around $1.72 million. Previously, the same entity had liquidated 101,000 ETH, held for five years, at an average price of $3,313, earning approximately $269 million in profit.

data.title: A whale who held UNI for five years sold off 2.493 million UNI tokens, making a profit of only $1.72 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Yu Jin, this morning, a whale (0x2C0...6098) that previously sold off 101,000 ETH (worth $334 million) at an average price of $3,313 and made a profit of $269 million, liquidated its UNI holdings, which it had held for 5 years. Three hours ago, this address transferred all 2.493 million UNI (worth $10.62 million) to Binance. This address bought UNI when it was listed in October 2020, with an average purchase price of $3.57. Today, it transferred all the shares to Binance at a price of $4.26, resulting in a profit of only $1.72 million over 5 years, a return of 19%. The ETH purchased by this address during the same period in 2020 yielded a 400% profit, while the UNI profit was only 19%.

data.title: Trump announced the nomination of Brett Matsumoto as the new director of the Bureau of Labor Statistics.
data.description: US President Donald Trump posted on social media that the Bureau of Labor Statistics, under the management of "weak and incompetent" officials, had for years misled American businesses, policymakers, and households with its "extremely inaccurate" data, leading to his dismissal of the former director. Trump stated, "I am delighted to nominate the brilliant Brett Matsumoto as the new director." Matsumoto, a former senior research economist at the Bureau and currently a senior economist on Trump's Council of Economic Advisers, believes Matsumoto is capable of "quickly addressing" the Bureau's long-standing problems on behalf of the American people, praising him as an "outstanding, reliable, and trustworthy economist" who will surely bring the Bureau "back to its former glory." (Jinshi)

data.title: Planet Morning News
data.description: 1. Trump: Nominates Kevin Warsh as Federal Reserve Chairman; 2. CZ: Listing tokens on Binance does not imply an obligation to control prices; 3. CZ: Binance does not participate in trading; accusations that Binance deliberately sold off tokens causing the 10.11 crash are baseless; 4. Gold experiences its biggest single-day drop in 40 years, silver falls over 36%, a record drop; 5. SEC and CFTC chairmen launch Project Crypto to unify crypto regulation; 6. White House Office of Management and Budget confirms partial government shutdown today; 7. Tether's 2025 net profit exceeds $10 billion, gold reserves exceed $17.4 billion; 8. Analyst: The precious metals feast is over, unprecedented and unparalleled; 9. Democratic senators call for a halt to Warsh's nomination until the Justice Department concludes its investigation of current Chairman Powell.

data.title: Bitcoin Mining Profitability Hits 14-Month Low Amid Price Drop
data.description:
Bitcoin's profitability for miners has reached its lowest point in 14 months, according to data from CryptoQuant. According to ChainCatcher, the miner profitability index has fallen to 21, marking the lowest level since November 2024.

CryptoQuant attributes this decline to a significant drop in Bitcoin prices this week, coupled with the current mining difficulty, resulting in extremely low miner revenues. Despite a consecutive five-cycle decrease in the Bitcoin network's hash rate, which is now at its lowest since September 2025, the situation remains challenging for miners.

In addition to the low revenue indicated by these metrics, some miners have been severely affected by a recent severe winter storm that swept across the eastern United States, causing ice and snow damage in multiple states.

data.title: Coinbase launches Infinex (INX) spot trading
data.description: According to official sources, Coinbase has announced the launch of spot trading for Infinex (INX). If liquidity conditions are met, the INX-USD trading pair will begin trading later today in supported regions.

data.title: MOLT Market Cap Experiences Significant Surge
data.description: MOLT's market capitalization has reached a peak of $124 million, currently standing at $81.49 million, marking a 24-hour increase of 2267.4%. According to Foresight News, this substantial growth highlights the volatility and rapid changes in the cryptocurrency market.

data.title: Tether's net profit is projected to exceed $10 billion by 2025, with gold reserves exceeding $17.4 billion.
data.description: Tether reported net profits exceeding $10 billion in 2025, primarily driven by growth in its USDT stablecoin and increased investments in U.S. Treasury bonds. The company held $6.3 billion in excess reserves at the end of the year to support its $186.5 billion USDT liabilities, making it one of the largest holders of U.S. government debt, with exposure reaching $141 billion. Its reserves also included $17.4 billion worth of gold and $8.4 billion worth of Bitcoin. (CoinDesk)

data.title: The US Senate passed a spending bill that would fund most departments of the federal government.
data.description: On March 30th local time, the U.S. Senate passed a spending bill to fund most departments of the federal government and sent it to the House of Representatives for consideration. Since House members are not in Washington and will not return until Monday (February 2nd), a Senate vote will inevitably result in a partial government shutdown beginning in the early hours of March 31st. House Speaker Mike Johnson stated in a private call with House Republicans that the most likely way for the House to pass the bill is through a fast-track vote on Monday night. (CCTV)
